head_commit.message:
fix: restore agent-fleet assembly-line scroll pin broken by doc-shell overflow (#882)

* fix: restore agent-fleet assembly-line scroll pin broken by doc-shell overflow

The /agent-system "Assembly Line" section pins via CSS position:sticky
inside a 340vh runway. Moving the page under the shared DocShell wrapper
introduced overflow-x:hidden on .doc-shell, which per spec forces
computed overflow-y to auto — making .doc-shell a scroll container. The
sticky panel then resolved against .doc-shell (which never scrolls
internally) instead of the viewport, so it never pinned and scrolled
away with the page.

Use overflow-x:clip instead: it clips horizontal overflow without
establishing a scroll container, so descendant sticky pins resolve
against the viewport again. Verified in-browser: pinned across the full
runway, belt scrubs horizontally, no horizontal scrollbar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: keep overflow-x hidden fallback under the clip on doc-shell

overflow:clip needs Safari 16+/Chrome 90+/Firefox 81+ and the repo pins
no browserslist baseline. Layer overflow-x:hidden before overflow-x:clip
so engines without clip keep today's clipping (last-valid-wins gives
modern engines clip + the sticky-pin fix).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
